description_long: This helicopter features a sleek, modern design with a streamlined fuselage and a prominent main rotor with four wide blades, each tipped with a distinctive red marking. The body is primarily dark gray with subtle metallic textures, accented by red highlights on the rotor tips, tail, and landing skids. The cockpit is compact and appears to have a single, slightly tinted window integrated into the front, providing visibility for the pilot. The tail boom is elongated and supports a small, horizontal tail rotor with two blades, also marked with red tips. The landing skids are robust, angular, and reinforced with crossbars, designed for stable landings on urban surfaces. Overall, the helicopter's design suggests a lightweight, agile craft suitable for rapid deployment in city environments.
description: A modern, dark gray helicopter with red accents, four-blade main rotor, compact cockpit, and sturdy angular landing skids. Features a streamlined body and a small tail rotor for urban maneuverability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide four-blade main rotor, compact cockpit, and angular landing skids.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the streamlined fuselage, elongated tail boom, and the two-blade tail rotor.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and symmetrical landing skids.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the main and tail rotors and landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four main rotor blades, wide and straight, made of composite material with dark gray bodies and red tips. Each blade is long, extending well beyond the fuselage, and features a slight taper at the ends.
attribute_tail_rotor: Two-blade tail rotor mounted horizontally at the end of the tail boom, with each blade featuring a red tip. The rotor is exposed with no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Angular, robust landing skids made of metal or composite, colored black with red highlights. The skids are reinforced with crossbars and have flat footpads for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Single, forward-facing cockpit window, slightly tinted and integrated into the front of the fuselage. The window is rectangular with rounded edges and minimal framing.
